title: polyalthia hispida
intro: Polyalthia hispida, also known as the Indian plum or Malabar plum, is a plant species in the Annonaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant's bark and roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Polyalthia hispida include the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and a treatment for digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, and is also used in traditional cooking as a spice and a flavoring agent.
